questions.text: An angry customer calls saying their transaction failed but their account was debited. What is your FIRST action?

options.text: Tell them to call back tomorrow when a manager is available
Apologize, assure them you will resolve it, and ask for their account number to investigate
Immediately refund the amount without checking
Ask them to come to the branch in person

questions.text: A customer is shouting at you in the branch. Other customers are watching. What do you do?

options.text: Shout back to assert yourself
Walk away and ignore them
Stay calm, lower your voice, and invite them to a private area to discuss the issue
Call security immediately

questions.text: A customer asks you a question you do not know the answer to. You should:

options.text: Make up a plausible answer
Tell them the question is not your department
Acknowledge you're unsure, and commit to finding the correct answer
Redirect them to another branch

questions.text: A long-term customer requests a service your organization doesn't offer. What is the best response?

options.text: Simply say no and end the conversation
Apologize, explain the limitation, and offer the closest alternative you can provide
Promise to add the service soon even if you can't
Suggest they go to a competitor

questions.text: Which of the following best describes good customer service?

options.text: Closing issues as fast as possible, regardless of resolution quality
Making the customer feel heard, respected, and helped
Following scripts exactly, even when they don't fit the situation
Referring all difficult customers to supervisors

questions.text: A customer complains that they received incorrect information from a colleague. What do you do?

options.text: Blame your colleague and distance yourself
Tell the customer their complaint is invalid
Apologize on behalf of the organization, clarify the correct information, and escalate if needed
Ask the customer to file a written complaint
